title: Oil prices crash after Trump cancels strikes — WTI $87.71 (-2%), Brent $90.38 (-3%)
snippet: U.S. crude oil closed at $87.71/bbl (-2%), Brent settled at $90.38 (-3%). In extended trading, WTI fell 3.9% to $86.51, Brent lost 4.2% to $89.15. Triggered by Trump abruptly cancelling airstrikes and claiming deal is near. Biggest single-day decline since June 9 mutual halt. Intraday initially spiked on Hormuz closure/IRGC attacks, then reversed sharply.
tag_reason: New oil price data for June 12; significant reversal from intraday spike to sharp decline; first Brent sub-$91 since mutual halt
